expectedOutcome.summary: two SubClassOf axioms lift to two Horn implications, transitive subsumption derivable via SLD

fixtures.intendedToCatch: lifter loses or duplicates SubClassOf chain semantics; emits reverse-direction implications; or silently collapses the chain to a single axiom

expectedOutcome.summary: EquivalentClasses lifts to two universals (mutual implication); DisjointWith lifts to a conjunction implying ⊥

fixtures.intendedToCatch: EquivalentClasses lifted as a single biconditional that decomposes incorrectly; DisjointWith lifted as a single negated atom rather than as the impossibility of co-membership

expectedOutcome.summary: someValuesFrom→existential conjunction; allValuesFrom→universal-implication; hasValue→fact via named individual

fixtures.intendedToCatch: someValuesFrom lifted without an existential witness; allValuesFrom lifted as an existential rather than a universal-implication; hasValue lifted as an existential rather than as a direct fact

expectedOutcome.summary: cardinality restrictions lift preserving count + property + (for QCR) onClass; per ADR-012 (architect-ratified Step 4 spec-binding cycle 2026-05-07; ratified in committed form at a469371) the projector reconstructs cardinality as native OWL Restriction with minCardinality / maxCardinality / cardinality fields under Direct Mapping. Round-trip is byte-clean; no LossSignature emission. Phase 2 Step 4b ships the n-tuple matcher per ADR-007 §7's lifted shapes.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: cardinality silently dropped; QCR onClass filter erased; counting semantics lifted via a wrong existentialisation that loses the count

expectedOutcome.summary: class assertion → unary fact; object property assertion → binary fact; data property assertion → binary fact preserving typed literal

fixtures.intendedToCatch: data property assertion silently dropping the datatype IRI to a bare string; object property assertion folding source/target into the wrong order; class assertion treated as TBox rather than ABox

expectedOutcome.summary: owl:sameAs and owl:differentFrom lift to facts under reserved predicate names; sameAs equivalence axiomatization (reflexivity/symmetry/transitivity) injected

fixtures.intendedToCatch: owl:sameAs / owl:differentFrom dropped as 'metadata'; equivalence axiomatization not injected (which would silently disable identity propagation through other predicates)

expectedOutcome.summary: Functional/Transitive/Symmetric/InverseOf lift to canonical FOL axioms; recursive characteristics rewritten with visited-ancestor cycle guards per ADR-011; SLD resolution terminates

fixtures.intendedToCatch: lifter emits naive transitive/symmetric rules without visited-ancestor guards (loops indefinitely); InverseOf lifted as a single-direction subPropertyOf; Functional axiom expressed in non-Horn form rather than the implication

expectedOutcome.summary: domain and range axioms lift to conditional universals (∀x,y. P(x,y) → Domain(x); symmetric for range); structural FOL-tree match per API §3.7.1.1 worked example
expectedOutcome.phase4Reactivation: at Phase 4, additional expectation: prov:Entity(project_alpha) and prov:Entity(project_beta) entailed via the conditional implications once PROV-O ARC is loaded

fixtures.intendedToCatch: domain/range axioms silently dropped or lifted as existential restrictions on the domain/range class — companion canary catches the wrong-translation, this fixture catches the right-shape regression

expectedOutcome.summary: blank-node-bearing class expression lifts with deterministic Skolem identifiers via rdf-canonize (RDFC-1.0); 100 lift runs produce byte-identical Skolem IDs

fixtures.intendedToCatch: non-canonical blank-node naming producing different Skolem IDs each run; determinism failure surfaced as byte-diff across runs

expectedOutcome.summary: wrong existentialised translation forbidden; lifted FOL state has no synthesized ∀x. Domain(x) → ∃y. P(x,y) axiom and no SubClassOf(Domain, ObjectSomeValuesFrom(P, owl:Thing)) on the OWL side; query ∃y. P(lonely_entity, y) returns 'undetermined' (open-world) rather than 'true' via fabricated Skolem successor

fixtures.intendedToCatch: wrong existentialisation of domain/range axioms — would entail a fabricated Skolem successor for any class member, producing false existential entailments the source did not assert

expectedOutcome.summary: identity propagation through other predicates: same_as(a,b) ∧ p(b,c) ⊨ p(a,c) — query worksAt(superman, dailyplanet) returns 'true' (not 'undetermined')

fixtures.intendedToCatch: lifter that lifts owl:sameAs to facts but fails to inject identity-aware predicate variants per spec §5.5.2 — queries on substituted-individual names silently return 'undetermined' rather than the entailed 'true' verdict

expectedOutcome.summary: three IRI input forms (full URI, CURIE, bare URI) of the same axiom lift to byte-identical FOL after stableStringify; canonical internal form is expanded full URI

fixtures.intendedToCatch: lifter that fails to expand CURIEs against the prefix table or stores prefix-shorthand-form internally, leaking surface form into the canonical FOL state and producing different lifted output for semantically-identical inputs

expectedOutcome.summary: five punted-construct cases (owl:hasKey, NegativeObjectPropertyAssertion, punning, faceted-datatype-restriction, annotation-on-annotation) each throw UnsupportedConstructError with the documented `construct` field set to the specific construct identifier

fixtures.intendedToCatch: lifter that silently accepts a spec §13.1 punted construct and emits degraded FOL — or throws a generic Error rather than the typed UnsupportedConstructError with the documented `construct` field

expectedOutcome.summary: BFO 2020 standard-OWL subset (5 SubClassOf + 1 DisjointWith + 1 Transitive + 1 InverseOf = 8 input axioms) lifts to 9 FOL axioms (the InverseOf decomposes to two implications per ADR-007 §4); each lifted axiom is semantically equivalent (modulo OFBT meta-typing-predicate elision) to the corresponding canonical axiom block in arc/upstream-canonical/owl-axiomatization.clif (Layer A per arc/upstream-canonical/README.md)
expectedOutcome.clifParityScope: Layer A only (OWL CLIF axiomatization). Layer B (BFO 2020 CLIF — Continuant/Occurrent/etc. domain content) is a Phase 4+ deliverable when ARC content + bfo-2020.clif vendoring land.
expectedOutcome.cumulativeCoverageNote: Phase 1 plants the OWL-axiomatization CLIF parity flag (Layer A); subsequent phase exits add Layer B citations against bfo-2020.clif as ARC modules ship

fixtures.intendedToCatch: lifter that produces FOL diverging from canonical OWL CLIF semantics (Layer A: arc/upstream-canonical/owl-axiomatization.clif) for the standard-OWL-construct subset. The divergence would be invisible to internal canary discipline alone (which checks the lifter against itself); external ground truth in the vendored canonical source is the discriminator. Catches: silently dropping a subClassOf, emitting DisjointWith with wrong arity, mis-encoding Transitive as a non-Horn axiom, omitting one direction of the InverseOf bidirectional pair.

expected_v0.1_verdict.clifCitationsStatus: Verified — all 8 citations confirmed against arc/upstream-canonical/owl-axiomatization.clif at Phase 1 exit Step 9.2 (2026-05-04)

clifGroundTruth.clifText: (forall (X Y) (iff (SubClassOf X Y) (and (Class X) (Class Y) (forall (z) (if (X z) (Y z))))))
clifGroundTruth.mappingNote: Canonical CLIF includes (Class X) and (Class Y) meta-typing predicates. OFBT lifts the canonical-form BODY only; meta-typing antecedents are elided per OFBT encoding choice (every IRI used in a class position is implicitly a Class — no separate Class reification). Sound w.r.t. OWL semantics for v0.1.

clifGroundTruth.clifAxiomRef: lines 1006-1015 (SubClassOf definition) — same canonical block as preceding entry
clifGroundTruth.clifText: (forall (X Y) (iff (SubClassOf X Y) (and (Class X) (Class Y) (forall (z) (if (X z) (Y z))))))
clifGroundTruth.mappingNote: Same canonical citation. Phase 1 verifies the lifter SubClassOf handler once; this fixture exercises it five times against different class-IRI pairs as a regression-density check.

clifGroundTruth.clifText: (forall (X Y) (iff (SubClassOf X Y) (and (Class X) (Class Y) (forall (z) (if (X z) (Y z))))))
clifGroundTruth.mappingNote: Same canonical citation; see preceding SubClassOf entries.

clifGroundTruth.clifText: (forall (X Y) (iff (SubClassOf X Y) (and (Class X) (Class Y) (forall (z) (if (X z) (Y z))))))
clifGroundTruth.mappingNote: Same canonical citation; see preceding SubClassOf entries.

clifGroundTruth.clifText: (forall (X Y) (iff (SubClassOf X Y) (and (Class X) (Class Y) (forall (z) (if (X z) (Y z))))))
clifGroundTruth.mappingNote: Same canonical citation; see preceding SubClassOf entries.

clifGroundTruth.clifText: (forall (X Y) (iff (DisjointClasses X Y) (and (Class X) (Class Y) (Disjoint X Y))))  ;; with auxiliary: (forall (X Y) (iff (Disjoint X Y) (forall (x ...s) (not (and (X x ...s) (Y ...s))))))
clifGroundTruth.mappingNote: Two-step canonical definition: DisjointClasses(X,Y) ↔ (Class X), (Class Y), Disjoint(X,Y); Disjoint(X,Y) ↔ ∀x. ¬(X(x) ∧ Y(x)). OFBT's lifted '(P ∧ Q) → ⊥' is the contrapositive of '¬(P ∧ Q)' — logically equivalent under classical FOL. Class meta-typing predicates elided as for SubClassOf entries.

clifGroundTruth.clifText: (forall (R) (iff (TransitiveObjectProperty R) (and (OWLObjectProperty R) (forall (x y z) (if (and (R x y) (R y z)) (R x z))))))
clifGroundTruth.mappingNote: Canonical CLIF includes (OWLObjectProperty R) meta-typing predicate. OFBT's lifted FOL ∀x,y,z. P(x,y) ∧ P(y,z) → P(x,z) is the canonical-form BODY universal-implication; meta-typing antecedent elided per same encoding-choice rationale as SubClassOf entries. Cycle-guarded SLD ingestion is the Phase 3 evaluator's concern per ADR-007 §1, NOT in the lifted FOL.

clifGroundTruth.clifText: (forall (R1 R2) (iff (InverseObjectProperties R1 R2) (and (OWLObjectProperty R1) (OWLObjectProperty R2) (forall (x y) (iff (R1 x y) (R2 y x))))))
clifGroundTruth.mappingNote: Canonical CLIF expresses the inverse relationship as a single iff. OFBT emits the bidirectional implication as a PAIR per ADR-007 §4 (fresh allocator per direction); two implications together are logically equivalent to the canonical iff. Two-axiom decomposition is OFBT's canonical encoding; iff is the canonical OWL CLIF encoding. Meta-typing predicates elided as for SubClassOf and TransitiveObjectProperty.

expectedOutcome.summary: ObjectComplementOf(C) class expression lifts to fol:Negation around the inner class's lifted form. SubClassOf(NotPerson, ObjectComplementOf(Person)) → ∀x. NotPerson(x) → ¬Person(x). Classical-FOL negation (not NAF); classical-vs-NAF discrimination is Phase 2 projector / Phase 3 evaluator concern per ADR-007 §1 layer separation.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: lifter drops ObjectComplementOf silently; lifts it as a positive atom on a synthesized predicate (semantic leak — fabricates a predicate the source did not declare); or routes complement to NAF rather than classical negation (a spec §1.1 third-axis violation that would surface only at Phase 3 evaluation, not at lift time).

expected_v0.1_verdict.smeOriginRef: B3 from Step 4 SME review (signed off as code-complete; coverage-gap closure deferred to Phase 1 exit Step 9.1)

expectedOutcome.summary: Projector-direct fixture: input is a FOL axioms array (not OWL ontology). Test runner dispatches via folToOwl(fixture.input), bypassing the lifter. Projection emits Annotated Approximation strategy with at least one LossSignature carrying lossType: 'naf_residue', relationIRI matching the negated-predicate IRI (KnownDriver), reason: 'negation_over_unbound_predicate', and a stable content-addressed @id matching /^ofbt:ls\/[0-9a-f]{64}$/. Associated RecoveryPayload preserves the FOL state for Phase 3 CWA-evaluator re-assessment.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: projector silently emitting SubClassOf(Person, ObjectComplementOf(KnownDriver)) as Direct Mapping (structurally valid OWL but loses the precautionary naf_residue discrimination signal); without the Loss Signature, downstream consumers cannot distinguish a lifter-derived classical negation (round-trip clean) from a hand-authored FOL state where NAF/CWA semantics may be intended (round-trip lossy under a CWA-evaluator at Phase 3)

phase3Reactivation.expectedOutcome: evaluate(folInput, query: 'KnownDriver(alice)?', closedPredicates: ['KnownDriver']) returns 'true' (CWA-derived); evaluate(folInput, query: 'KnownDriver(alice)?') under default OWA returns 'undetermined'. The projection's RecoveryPayload preserves the FOL state needed for the CWA evaluation; re-lifting the projected OWL alone (under default OWA) cannot reach the CWA-derived 'true' verdict.
phase3Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If real evaluate() under OWA returns 'true' (matching the CWA evaluation) where the stub-equivalent and the spec §6.3 default-OWA contract say 'undetermined', ESCALATE: open-world-preservation broken (a CWA assumption leaked through projection without the Loss Signature flagging it).

acceptanceCriteria: Phase 2 entry packet §3.2 + architect Q-G ruling 2026-05-07 (+1 fixture authorized)

expectedOutcome.summary: Projector-direct fixture: input is a single FOL binary atom (ObjectPropertyAssertion-shaped). Test runner dispatches via folToOwl(fixture.input). Projection emits Direct Mapping ObjectPropertyAssertion(uncatalogedRelation, alice, bob) AND a single informational LossSignature carrying lossType: 'unknown_relation', relationIRI matching the predicate IRI, reason: 'predicate_iri_not_in_loaded_arc_modules', and a stable content-addressed @id matching /^ofbt:ls\/[0-9a-f]{64}$/. No RecoveryPayload — Direct Mapping output is the full recovery.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: projector silently emitting Direct Mapping output WITHOUT the informational unknown_relation Loss Signature when the predicate IRI has no ARC-content backing; without the Loss Signature, downstream consumers cannot distinguish a predicate with full ARC-content semantic backing from a predicate that round-tripped structurally but has no semantic interpretation machinery available; Phase 4 strict-mode operation per spec §3.3 requires this discrimination as a hard contract; Phase 2's informational emission lays the groundwork

phase4Reactivation.expectedOutcome: Under Phase 4 strict-mode operation per spec §3.3, the predicate IRI http://example.org/p2-uncatalogued/uncatalogedRelation either (a) is loaded as ARC content (this fixture's namespace promoted to a test ARC module) and round-trips cleanly without unknown_relation emission, OR (b) the projector throws a documented strict-mode rejection error per spec §3.3's rejection contract. Phase 4 entry packet picks (a) or (b).
phase4Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If Phase 4 strict mode silently emits informational unknown_relation (Phase 2 behavior) where the strict-mode contract requires either ARC loading OR rejection, ESCALATE: strict-mode promotion not propagated from spec §3.3 to projector implementation.

expectedOutcome.summary: Lift+project round-trip preserves the ObjectIntersectionOf anonymous class expression with both Restriction sub-expressions (someValuesFrom + allValuesFrom) intact. The projector reconstructs the class-expression hierarchy via Step 3b's recursive descent without inventing fresh b-node IRIs. Projected output is structurally-equivalent to input modulo OWL serialization convention and placeholder manifest fields.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: the projector silently dropping one of the ObjectIntersectionOf sub-expressions (emitting a single Restriction instead of the full intersection); collapsing the intersection to ObjectUnionOf (semantic shift); inventing a fresh b-node IRI for the anonymous ObjectIntersectionOf when the source ontology used inline nesting; or losing the property+filler structure of either Restriction sub-expression

phase5Reactivation.expectedOutcome: Once Step 5 ships explicit per-axiom strategy reporting, this fixture's assertion strengthens to: strategy chosen for the SubClassOf axiom = 'direct'. The Annotated Approximation fallback path is NOT triggered (no Loss Signature emitted).
phase5Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If Step 5's strategy router reports strategy != 'direct' (e.g., 'annotated-approximation' would mean Direct Mapping is incorrectly failing for this case), ESCALATE: regression in Step 3b's class-expression reconstruction.

expectedOutcome.summary: Lift+project round-trip across 8 OWL 2 DL constructs (SubClassOf, EquivalentClasses, ClassAssertion, ObjectPropertyAssertion, Symmetric, Transitive, ObjectPropertyDomain, ObjectPropertyRange) preserves all structural shape under Direct Mapping. No Loss Signatures emitted; no Recovery Payloads emitted. Projected ontology contains the same 8 axioms as input modulo OWL serialization convention + placeholder manifest fields.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: the projector silently routing any of the 8 input axioms to Annotated Approximation (would emit at least one Loss Signature when none is expected); OR routing to a non-existent third strategy that produces structurally-similar but semantically-shifted output; OR pair-matching incorrectly collapsing two non-converse SubClassOf axioms into a spurious EquivalentClasses; OR single-axiom Direct Mapping silently dropping one of the 4 ABox / 4 RBox axioms

phase5Reactivation.expectedOutcome: Once Step 5 ships explicit per-axiom strategy reporting, this fixture's assertion strengthens to: for each of the 8 input axioms, the recorded strategy is 'direct'. Property-Chain Realization (Step 6) and Annotated Approximation (Step 4) MUST NOT be invoked for any of the 8 axioms.
phase5Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If Step 5's strategy router reports strategy != 'direct' for any of the 8 axioms, ESCALATE: strategy-routing correctness regression. The wrong strategy choice is a Ring 2 pass that hides a real bug per Phase 2 entry packet §3.7 + §10.2 banked principles.

expectedOutcome.summary: Projector-direct fixture exercising BOTH Annotated-Approximation-routing trigger paths simultaneously (naf_residue + unknown_relation). Expected newLossSignatures.length >= 2 with one naf_residue (severity rank 2) and one unknown_relation (severity rank 5); severity-ordering contract preserved when consumers sort by LOSS_SIGNATURE_SEVERITY_ORDER. All @id fields match /^ofbt:ls\/[0-9a-f]{64}$/ per ADR-011. Annotated Approximation strategy is the routing destination for both trigger paths.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: the projector emitting only ONE LossSignature when TWO are expected (silent drop of one trigger path); OR conflating naf_residue and unknown_relation into a single LossSignature; OR violating the severity-ordering contract when consumers request ordered Loss Signatures (severity rank 2 must precede rank 5 in LOSS_SIGNATURE_SEVERITY_ORDER); OR routing one trigger path to Annotated Approximation while routing the other through some other strategy

expected_v0.1_verdict.noteOnEmissionCount: Step 4a smoke-test 2026-05-07 emits 4 LossSignatures for this fixture (the Person predicate in the same namespace also triggers unknown_relation, plus the negated KnownProfession + the unfamiliarBond predicate). The contract is at-least-2 with both lossType members present; exact count varies with the projector's emission policy for predicate IRIs in the fixture's uncatalogued namespace. Step 5's strategy router formalization may tighten this contract.

phase5Reactivation.expectedOutcome: Once Step 5 ships explicit per-axiom strategy reporting, this fixture's assertion strengthens to: for both input axioms, the recorded strategy is 'annotated-approximation'.
phase5Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If Step 5's strategy router reports strategy != 'annotated-approximation' for the naf_residue axiom, ESCALATE: strategy-routing correctness regression.

acceptanceCriteria: §12 round-trip parity (Layer A canonical CLIF semantics preserved)
Phase 1 exit forward-track §9 (corpus-side Layer A round-trip)

expectedOutcome.summary: Lift+project round-trip across the BFO 2020 standard-OWL subset (8 axioms: 5 SubClassOf + 1 DisjointWith + 1 TransitiveObjectProperty + 1 InverseObjectProperties) preserves all Layer A canonical semantics. Lifted FOL matches p1_bfo_clif_classical verbatim (9 axioms after InverseObjectProperties decomposition); projected OWL structurally-equivalent to input modulo serialization convention. fixtures.intendedToCatch: the projector implementing a non-canonical FOL → OWL mapping that round-trips structurally but encodes different OWL semantics than the Layer A canonical form dictates; OR pair-matching incorrectly re-collapsing TransitiveObjectProperty + InverseObjectProperties into a single axiom; OR silently dropping the DisjointWith axiom; OR introducing meta-typing predicates that the elision discipline (ADR-007 §10) explicitly omits

acceptanceCriteria: Q-Step6-3 ruling (Step 6 projector-only; lifter ObjectPropertyChain support deferred)

expectedOutcome.summary: Projector-direct fixture: input is a single FOL axiom representing the canonical 2-property chain pattern (∀x,y,z. parent(x,y) ∧ parent(y,z) → grandparent(x,z)). Test runner dispatches via folToOwl(fixture.input). Projection emits Property-Chain Realization strategy with one RBox axiom of shape ObjectPropertyChain { chain: [parent, parent], superProperty: grandparent } AND one RecoveryPayload carrying approximationStrategy: 'PROPERTY_CHAIN', originalFOL preserving the input verbatim, and scopeNotes containing 'regularity_scope_warning' per architect Q-Step6-1 ruling. No LossSignatures emitted (chain projection is reversible-regime, not loss).

fixtures.intendedToCatch: the projector silently routing the chain pattern to Annotated Approximation when spec §6.1.2 + §6.2 specifies Property-Chain Realization as Tier-3 BEFORE Annotated Approximation as Tier-default; OR routing to Direct Mapping (would emit a SubObjectPropertyOf without the chain decomposition); OR emitting ObjectPropertyChain WITHOUT the Recovery Payload's regularity_scope_warning (would silently assert regularity the system cannot verify, violating spec §6.2.1's literal framing per architect Q-Step6-1 ruling); OR collapsing the 2-property chain to a single property; OR introducing a phantom property in the chain that the source FOL did not declare

phase4Reactivation.expectedOutcome: Under Phase 4 ARC content loaded with regularityCheck(A, importClosure) active, this fixture's chain pattern either (a) emits ObjectPropertyChain WITHOUT regularity_scope_warning if regularity is verified under import closure, OR (b) falls through to Annotated Approximation per spec §6.2 algorithm if regularity fails. The warning's optional-rather-than-mandatory transition is a non-breaking strengthening of the Phase 2 contract per ADR-011's Q-3 banked principle.
phase4Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If Phase 4's regularityCheck(A, importClosure) is active AND emits regularity_scope_warning for THIS fixture (whose chain should be regularity-confirmed under reasonable test ARC configuration), ESCALATE: regularity-check semantics regression between Phase 2 and Phase 4.

expectedOutcome.summary: Strategy-routing canary asserting Tier-3 dispatch per spec §6.2's tiered fallthrough algorithm. Same 2-property chain FOL shape as p2_property_chain_realization_simplified (sibling fixture); discrimination is in assertion focus. Step 5's strategy router records strategy='property-chain' for the chain axiom; Step 6's chain detection emits ObjectPropertyChain { chain: [parent, parent], superProperty: grandparent } with chain array order EXACTLY preserving the FOL binary-atom sequence. Recovery Payload carries regularity_scope_warning per architect Q-Step6-1 ruling.
expectedOutcome.discriminationFromSibling: asserts strategy choice + ObjectPropertyChain.chain array exactness; sibling p2_property_chain_realization_simplified asserts round-trip + Recovery Payload preservation

fixtures.intendedToCatch: strategy router silently routing chain axiom to non-Tier-3 strategy ('direct' would emit SubObjectPropertyOf without chain decomposition; 'annotated-approximation' would emit LossSignatures when the chain is reversible-regime-projectable; 'unmatched' would skip emission); OR Step 6's chain detection emitting ObjectPropertyChain with WRONG chain array order (P_1 ∘ P_2 ≠ P_2 ∘ P_1 in general; order preservation is load-bearing); OR collapsing 2-property chain to single-element [parent]; OR introducing phantom property in chain array

expected_v0.1_verdict.expectedChainArrayOrderExactness: chain array order EXACTLY [parent, parent] (preserving FOL binary-atom sequence)

phase4Reactivation.expectedOutcome: Under Phase 4's regularityCheck(A, importClosure): (a) regularity-confirmed → strategy stays 'property-chain' AND regularity_scope_warning CLEARED from Recovery Payload; (b) irregular → strategy fallthrough to 'annotated-approximation' per spec §6.2 algorithm.
phase4Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If Phase 4's regularity check is active AND strategy router still reports 'property-chain' WITH regularity_scope_warning preserved for a chain that the import closure verifies as regular, ESCALATE: regularity-check semantics did not propagate from spec §6.2.1 Phase 4 contract to strategy router's emission decision.

acceptanceCriteria: Architect Q3 ruling — stub-evaluator harness contract + Phase 3 re-exercise gate

expectedOutcome.summary: Round-trip preservation of an entailed query. Lift+project+re-lift of the OWL input (3 axioms: 2 SubClassOf chain + 1 ClassAssertion) produces F_3 byte-equivalent to F_1. Stub-evaluator on the discriminating query Person(alice)? returns 'true' on both F_1 and F_3 via backward-chain through the SubClassOf-derived rules (Mother → Female → Person) terminating at Mother(alice). Failure mode: projector silently drops one of the SubClassOf rules → F_3 lacks the entailment chain → stub returns 'undetermined' on F_3 instead of 'true' → canary fires.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: the projector producing a round-tripped OWL surface where a previously-entailed query Q silently degrades to 'undetermined' (lost-entailment failure mode); specifically: silently dropping SubClassOf axioms via buggy pair-matching that mis-collapses two unrelated SubClassOf into a spurious EquivalentClasses, OR dropping one SubClassOf during class-expression reconstruction recursion, OR similar entailment-chain-breaking projector bugs

phase3Reactivation.expectedOutcome: evaluate(F_3, query: 'Person(alice)?') returns 'true' (must match stub) — real evaluator's Horn-resolution chain through the two SubClassOf-derived rules terminating at Mother(alice) is identical to the stub's backward-chain trace.
phase3Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If real evaluate() returns 'undetermined' where the stub returned 'true', ESCALATE: round-trip lost the SubClassOf-chain entailment between Phase 2 and Phase 3. If real returns 'true' (matching stub), NORMAL — Phase 3's evaluator handles this case at least as well as the stub did.

acceptanceCriteria: Architect Q3 ruling — stub-evaluator harness contract + Phase 3 re-exercise gate

expectedOutcome.summary: Round-trip preservation of an OWA-undetermined query. Lift+project+re-lift of the minimal OWL input (2 ClassAssertions, no rules, no Knows facts) produces F_3 byte-equivalent to F_1. Stub-evaluator on the discriminating query Knows(alice, bob)? returns 'undetermined' on both F_1 and F_3 (default OWA per spec §6.3 — no proof of Knows(alice, bob), no proof of its negation). Failure mode: projector silently introduces a closure annotation OR a fabricated negative atom → F_3 derives ¬Knows under CWA semantics → stub returns 'false' on F_3 instead of 'undetermined' → canary fires (open-world preservation broken).

fixtures.intendedToCatch: the projector silently introducing a closure annotation OR a fabricated negative atom in the OWL surface that re-lifts to a CWA-derivable negation, collapsing the discriminating query Knows(alice, bob)? from 'undetermined' (default OWA per spec §6.3) to 'false' (CWA-derived); specific failure modes: spurious rdfs:isDefinedBy or similar closure annotation; phantom NegativeObjectPropertyAssertion not in the input; mis-lifted punted construct that should reject but instead silently emits classical negation

phase3Reactivation.expectedOutcome: evaluate(F_3, query: 'Knows(alice, bob)?') under default OWA (NO closedPredicates) returns 'undetermined' (must match stub; CWA-collapse forbidden). If a Phase 3 consumer explicitly passes closedPredicates: ['knows'], the real evaluator MAY return 'false' — but that is consumer-opt-in CWA, NOT a round-trip-introduced CWA leak.
phase3Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If real evaluate() under default OWA (no closedPredicates) returns 'false' where the stub returned 'undetermined', ESCALATE: open-world-preservation broken — a CWA assumption leaked through the round-trip without consumer-side opt-in. This is the load-bearing canary failure the discipline exists to catch.

acceptanceCriteria: Phase 2 entry packet §3.7 + §10.8 (cross-section defense-pair refinement architect-banked 2026-05-06)
Architect Q3 ruling — stub-evaluator harness contract + Phase 3 re-exercise gate

expectedOutcome.summary: Cross-section defense-in-depth canary half (sibling: strategy_routing_direct in §3.3). Engineered such that the round-trip's graph-shape comparison reports clean (auxiliary facts about bob ground the entailment witnesses regardless of class-expression quantifier kind). At Phase 2 stub-fidelity, the canary asserts baseline preservation: round-trip OWL surface byte-equivalent, no spurious Loss Signatures (mis-routing-to-Annotated-Approximation detection), no spurious Recovery Payloads, stub queries return 'true' on both F_1 and F_3. Phase 3 reactivation surfaces the engineered kind-swap detection via closedPredicates: ['hasChild'] discriminating query.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: the projector silently mis-routing the Direct-Mapping-eligible SubClassOf-with-someValuesFrom-Restriction axiom to Annotated Approximation (would emit Loss Signature + Recovery Payload when the round-trip is structurally clean and Direct Mapping is the correct strategy); OR mis-routing to Property-Chain Realization (would emit ObjectPropertyChain + Recovery Payload + change RBox axiom count); OR mis-routing to a non-existent third strategy that produces structurally-similar-but-semantically-shifted output. The audit-artifact assertions catch (1) and (2). The kind-swap detection is Phase 3 territory (phase3Reactivation field documents that re-exercise).

expected_v0.1_verdict.crossSectionDefensePairAssertion: paired with strategy_routing_direct (positive half) per architect-banked §10.8 cross-section pattern

phase3Reactivation.expectedOutcome: Discriminating Phase 3 query under closedPredicates: ['hasChild', 'Person']: evaluate(F_3, query: '∃y. hasChild(alice, y) ∧ ¬Person(y)?', closedPredicates: ['hasChild', 'Person']) — under correct someValuesFrom round-trip, evaluates to 'false' (the only y with hasChild(alice, y) is bob; Person(bob) is closed and asserted; ¬Person(bob) is 'false' under CWA). Under buggy allValuesFrom mis-routing, F_3 has different structural semantics; Phase 3's evaluator surfaces the kind-swap.
phase3Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If real evaluate() divergence in EITHER direction on the discriminating query, ESCALATE: the engineered semantic shift is now visible to the real evaluator and the round-trip's wrongness is confirmed (round-trip is buggy — projector mis-routed the class-expression's quantifier kind). Alternatively, if Phase 3's evaluator gives the same answer regardless of kind-swap, the canary needs STRENGTHENING — the engineered case wasn't actually detectable at Phase 3 fidelity either, and the canary's design needs revision before Phase 4 ARC content lands.

acceptanceCriteria: Phase 2 entry packet §3.3 (architect-ratified 2026-05-06 — 4th of 4 strategy-routing fixtures)
ADR-012 banked principle 2 (fixture regime annotations are provisional; SME annotation-tracking amendment 2026-05-XX)

expectedOutcome.summary: Projector-direct fixture documenting Phase 2 no-strategy-applicable BASELINE behavior. Input is a bare top-level fol:False axiom — no Direct Mapping pattern matches, no Property-Chain Realization pattern matches; strategy router falls through to Tier-default Annotated Approximation per Step 5's tiered-fallthrough algorithm. Phase 2 projector treats this as silent-fallthrough with EMPTY OUTPUT (no axioms emitted; no LossSignature; no RecoveryPayload). Spec §0.1 conformance gap documented; closure deferred to Phase 3 entry packet ratification (throw discipline OR always-emit-LossSignature — see phase3Reactivation field).
expectedOutcome.phase2SpecConformanceGap: spec §0.1 'every axiom emits a LossSignature' contract violated; closure forward-tracked to Phase 3 entry packet

fixtures.intendedToCatch: REGRESSION from Phase 2's documented silent-fallthrough baseline. The canary's role at Phase 2 is regression-detection: the silent-fallthrough is the BASELINE; any DEVIATION (toward stricter throw, toward LossSignature emission, toward mis-routed structurally-invalid output) signals a Phase 2 implementation change that requires architect re-routing per ADR-012's behavioral-contract evolution discipline.

expected_v0.1_verdict.note: test runner asserts folToOwl(fixture.input) RETURNS a value (does NOT throw). Returned value's ontology axiom counts all 0; newLossSignatures + newRecoveryPayloads also empty per Phase 2 baseline. If projector throws at Phase 2, test FAILS — diagnostic-throw is forward-tracked to Phase 3, not Phase 2 implementation.

phase3Reactivation.expectedOutcome: Architect ratified Q-3-C 2026-05-08 — Option (a) Throw discipline. Phase 3 Step 8 ships NoStrategyAppliesError (13th typed error class in API §10) + no_strategy_applies (17th reason code per ADR-011 banked principle 2 + spec §11.2 minor-version-bump discipline). Phase 2's silent-fallthrough path amends to throw NoStrategyAppliesError. Fixture's contract at Phase 3 reactivation: folToOwl(fixture.input) throws NoStrategyAppliesError; expectedOutcomeOptionA becomes the live contract; expectedOutcomeOptionB documented as the rejected alternative for audit-trail completeness.
phase3Reactivation.divergenceTrigger: If Phase 3 Step 8 implementation does NOT throw NoStrategyAppliesError on this fixture's input, ESCALATE: Q-3-C ratified Option (a) Throw discipline is not honored. Phase 2 exit packet update per cycle-2 Pass 2b I6 records the closure reference.

expectedOutcome.summary: checkConsistency on the lifted FOL state returns consistent: false. Witness chain names ParadoxClass + the EquivalentClasses-with-ObjectComplementOf axiom. Decidable in Horn-checkable fragment per spec §8.5.1. unverifiedAxioms empty.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: checkConsistency silently returning consistent: true on the canonical equivalent-to-complement contradiction. Failure modes: Skolem-witness assertion not introduced; EquivalentClasses unfolding incomplete; resolution depth bound terminates before contradiction reached.

acceptanceCriteria: spec §8.5.1 Horn-checkable fragment (excludes arbitrary disjunctive class expressions)

expectedOutcome.summary: checkConsistency returns consistent: 'undetermined' with reason: 'coherence_indeterminate'. unverifiedAxioms populated with the SubClassOf-with-ObjectUnionOf-consequent axiom (the SOLE non-Horn axiom; the DisjointClasses is Horn-expressible per spec §8.5.1 and not part of the non-Horn-fragment-escape, per Q-Frank-Step9-A Ask 2 ruling 2026-05-10). Classical FOL judges KB inconsistent; v0.1's Horn-only check correctly reports 'undetermined' rather than silently passing.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: checkConsistency silently returning consistent: true when input requires disjunctive case-analysis the Horn fragment cannot perform. Discrimination: returning 'undetermined' with empty unverifiedAxioms also fails (violates spec §8.5.5); surfacing the Horn-expressible DisjointClasses(A, D) in unverifiedAxioms also fails (violates API §8.1.1 — would conflate transitively-implicating Horn axioms with non-Horn axioms per Q-Frank-Step9-A Ask 2 ruling 2026-05-10).

acceptanceCriteria: ROADMAP discrimination requirement: different reason+unverifiedAxioms than disjunctive sibling

expectedOutcome.summary: checkConsistency returns consistent: 'undetermined' with reason: 'coherence_indeterminate'. unverifiedAxioms contains the existential SubClassOf, universal SubClassOf, and ObjectComplementOf SubClassOf axioms — the three axioms whose interaction requires existential-witnessing the Horn fragment cannot perform. Discriminates from disjunctive sibling: NOT ObjectUnionOf shape.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: checkConsistency silently returning consistent: true when the input's inconsistency requires existential-witnessing. Plus: returning 'undetermined' with unverifiedAxioms only naming disjunctive shapes (failure to detect existential as Horn-escape).

acceptanceCriteria: ROADMAP Phase 3 No-Collapse Adversarial Corpus — silent-pass catchall canary

expectedOutcome.summary: checkConsistency MUST NOT return consistent: true. Acceptable verdicts: 'undetermined' with populated unverifiedAxioms OR consistent: false (only if tableau-extended implementation ships pre-Phase-3-exit). KB classically inconsistent through compounded non-Horn pathways: disjunctive partition + existential-witnessing + property-chain + inverse.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: the canonical SILENT-PASS failure mode for No-Collapse Guarantee: checkConsistency returning consistent: true on a KB classically inconsistent through non-Horn machinery. Catchall regression-density check; does not assert specific verdict (false vs 'undetermined' both acceptable) but asserts consistent: true is wrong.

expectedOutcome.summary: checkConsistency(session, axiomSet) returns consistent: false. Hypothetical DisjointClasses(Adult, Person) contradicts base Adult ⊑ Person + Adult(alice). Witnesses name the axiomSet axiom and the base axioms it contradicted. Subsequent checkConsistency(session) returns base-KB consistent: true (non-persistence guarantee).

fixtures.intendedToCatch: checkConsistency failing to participate axiomSet (would return base-KB consistent: true ignoring hypothetical); persisting axiomSet (corrupts session); witnesses incomplete (failing to name the hypothetical's contribution).

expectedOutcome.summary: checkConsistency(session, axiomSet) returns consistent: 'undetermined' with reason 'coherence_indeterminate'. unverifiedAxioms contains the hypothetical SubClassOf-with-ObjectUnionOf axiom only — base KB axioms NOT flagged (correct attribution to hypothetical extension).

fixtures.intendedToCatch: checkConsistency mis-attributing Horn-incompleteness to base axioms; returning consistent: false on satisfiable KB; silently consistent: true ignoring the Horn-fragment escape.

acceptanceCriteria: ROADMAP Phase 3 Hypothetical-Axiom Set — positive control

expectedOutcome.summary: checkConsistency(session, axiomSet) returns consistent: true. Hypothetical ClassAssertion(Mother, alice) is consistent with base Person(alice) + Mother ⊑ Person. Horn-decidable; positive control for the test set.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: checkConsistency over-pessimistically returning 'undetermined' on a clean Horn-decidable extension; incorrectly returning consistent: false; conflating new content with new uncertainty.

acceptanceCriteria: ROADMAP Phase 3 Hypothetical-Axiom Set — non-persistence verification

expectedOutcome.summary: Two-call protocol against same session. Call 1: checkConsistency(session, axiomSet) returns consistent: false. Call 2: checkConsistency(session) returns consistent: true (hypothetical did not persist; base-KB verdict). Discriminating assertion: Call 2's verdict must be base-KB.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: non-persistence violation: hypothetical axiomSet persisting in session after checkConsistency(session, axiomSet). Failure modes: assertz without matching retract; derived facts not retracted; shared-reference mutation instead of copy-and-extend.

acceptanceCriteria: Phase 3 entry packet §3.5 step-N-bind (architect-ratified 2026-05-08 Q-3-E) — original binding
Phase 4 entry packet §3.2 corpus-before-code re-binding (architect-ratified 2026-05-10 Q-4-B)
spec §5.4 cycle detection + ADR-013 visited-ancestor list (Class 3 cycle-prone predicate)

expectedOutcome.summary: Class 3 cycle-prone-predicate fixture (recursive SubClassOf chain via mutual EquivalentClasses). Re-bound to Phase 4 corpus-before-code per Q-4-B architect ruling 2026-05-10 because BFO ARC content surfaces the EquivalentClasses-cycle forcing case at lift time. Phase 4 implementation closes Class 3 at Step 7 (lifter ObjectPropertyChain support per Q-Step6-3) OR Step 6 (regularityCheck activation per spec §6.2.1).

fixtures.intendedToCatch: cycle detection failure: infinite loop on EquivalentClasses cycle; depth-bound termination misreported as closure_truncated; thrown when consumer config requested non-throwing.

acceptanceCriteria: ROADMAP §3.4 Phase 3 No-Collapse Adversarial Corpus (BFO-gated; activated at Phase 4)
spec §8.5.1 Horn-checkable fragment (simple disjointness Horn-checkable per Q-Frank-Step9-A banking)
spec §8.5.2 FOLFalse-in-head inconsistency proof outcome ordering

expectedOutcome.summary: checkConsistency on lifted FOL with BFO 2020 ARC loaded returns consistent: 'false'. BFO Disjointness Map declares Continuant ⊓ Occurrent disjoint; alice asserted to both; Step 7 FOLFalse-in-head proof fires; Horn-checkable per spec §8.5.1.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: checkConsistency silently returning consistent: 'true' on canonical BFO Disjointness Map firing (Continuant ⊓ Occurrent on alice). Specific failure modes: ARC loader fails to lift Disjointness Map as FOLFalse-in-head; Step 7 proof fails on Layer-B subsumption-derived bodies; mis-classifies disjointness as non-Horn (this fixture's body is TRIGGERED by alice's dual assertion, so rejection direction works in v0.1 regardless of the Layer A consistency-affirmation gap forward-tracked to v0.2).
fixtures.expected_v0.2_elk_verdict: consistent: 'false' identical (ELK EL profile handles disjointness directly)

acceptanceCriteria: spec §3.4.1 Connected With as primitive + bridge axiom

expectedOutcome.summary: Phase 4 lift with BFO 2020 ARC loaded produces lifted FOL containing: (1) Connected With as primitive predicate atom, (2) one-way bridge axiom overlaps → connected_with, (3) NO reverse-direction implication (the symmetric/biconditional emission per ADR-007 §4 decomposition is detected by the forbidden pattern's reverse-direction-implication test).

fixtures.intendedToCatch: Phase 4 lifter's silent collapse of Connected With to defined-as-overlap form (corrupts BFO mereotopological asymmetry); incomplete bridge axiom (Connected With present but no implication relating it to overlap); symmetric biconditional emission detected via reverse-direction-implication test per ADR-007 §4 decomposition.

acceptanceCriteria: Q-Frank-4 publication discipline (per-canary catchall MUST-NOT-be-true assertion pattern)

expectedOutcome.summary: checkConsistency on lifted FOL with BFO ARC loaded MUST NOT return consistent: 'true'. KB has multiple paths to BFO Disjointness contradiction (direct + subsumption + part_of). Acceptable: 'false' OR 'undetermined' with populated unverifiedAxioms.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: canonical SILENT-PASS failure mode for No-Collapse Guarantee against BFO Layer B content. Catches: implementation that runs Horn resolution and silently returns 'true'; Step 7 proof failing on subsumption chains; detection of direct path but not indirect paths through subsumption or part_of.
fixtures.expected_v0.2_elk_verdict: MUST-NOT-be-true assertion holds; v0.2 ELK + tableau extensions close any non-Horn pathways through subsumption chains

fixtures.intendedToCatch: closedPredicates failure: parameter ignored; CWA applied globally; wrong reason code.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: OWA-default failure: CWA applied globally returning 'false' instead of 'undetermined'; wrong reason code (e.g., unbound_predicate instead of open_world_undetermined per Q-3-Step4-A Option γ refusal — OWA-stance semantic, not predicate-state semantic).

acceptanceCriteria: Phase 4 entry packet §3.3 step-N-bind + corpus addition transparency (Q-4-Step4-A architect ratification 2026-05-11; mid-phase architectural-gap micro-cycle)
Q-4-Step4-A.1 N-ary semantics ruling: emitter expands N-ary to pairwise binary axioms
Q-4-Step4-A.2 simple disjointness only ruling: DisjointUnion deferred to v0.2 / future-phase
arcEntries: BFO Disjointness Map (11 disjointnessAxioms in arc/core/bfo-2020.json per Q-4-Step4-A)

expectedOutcome.summary: Lift of an empty input ontology with BFO 2020 ARC module loaded produces lifted FOL containing pairwise binary universal-implication axioms derived from the BFO Disjointness Map per Q-4-Step4-A.1 emitter implementation note. Spot-checked: binary Continuant_Occurrent_root (1 pairwise axiom) + ternary Continuant_subkinds IC/SDC/GDC (3 pairwise axioms) + 4-ary Occurrent_subkinds Process/PB/TR/STR (2 of 6 pairs sampled). 6 requiredPatterns must match; 2 forbiddenPatterns must NOT match.

fixtures.intendedToCatch: Phase 4 Step 4 BFO Disjointness Map axiom emission failure modes: (1) lifter loads disjointnessAxioms field but fails to emit any FOL axioms; (2) lifter emits N-ary single-axiom shape (3-conjunct on ternary disjointnessAxiom) instead of pairwise-expanded binary shape per Q-4-Step4-A.1 implementation note; (3) lifter emits each pair twice (forward + reverse direction) corrupting determinism + Step-7 proof structure; (4) lifter emits the wrong False-in-head shape.
